description:
22.11 falls back to indirect dispatch when the closure Var's Descr is a union of multiple closure_lits (e.g., \`if cond, do: double, else: neg\`). This ticket extends the direct path to cover that case when the members share compatible ABIs.

## Scope

Gated on demonstrated demand. File a fixture that exercises a union-of-singletons closure flow (e.g., conditional closure binding into apply2) and measure whether the existing indirect fallback regresses anything we care about. If yes, implement:

Option α (lightweight): when all union members resolve to body specs with identical param_reprs and return_reprs, emit a direct return_call to ONE member (any member — they're ABI-compatible) using cl as self. Reads captures via cl+24 — captures may differ per member but each member's body reads its own; provided the body still indexes correctly.

Option β (full switch): emit a Cranelift br_table on the resolved body fp identifier, branching to each member's direct return_call. Cost: code size; gain: full precision.

Option γ (fall-through): keep indirect path; close ticket as not-worth-it. Document the perf rationale.

## Acceptance

- Decision documented in ticket comments before implementation.
- If implemented, cargo test --workspace green; fz dump on the new fixture shows direct (or switched) dispatch.
- If γ: close as won't-fix with rationale.

## BLOCKED BY

- fz-ul4.27.22.11 [[fz-cps-seam.codegen-direct]]
title: fz-cps-seam.union-singletons: direct dispatch for union of closure_lits
